CTN 4, 004 [Enuma Anu Enlil 08][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
o i 1o i 1

* 30 ina IGI.LA₂-šu₂ TUR₃ NIGIN₂ KUR!(ŠE) DU₃.A.⸢BI⸣ x x-šu₂ [...]

(o i 1) (If) the Moon at its appearance is surrounded by a halo: the land, all of it, ... [...].

o i 22

* 30 ina IGI.LA₂-šu₂ 1 TUR₃ NIGIN₂ KUR x x BE

(o i 2) (If) the Moon at its appearance is surrounded by 1 halo: the land ....

o i 33

* 30 ina IGI.LA₂-šu₂ 2 TUR₃ NIGIN₂ KUR GAR x GAR [...]

(o i 3) (If) the Moon at its appearance is surrounded by 2 halos: the land ... [...].

o i 44

* 30 ina IGI.LA₂-šu₂ 3 TUR₃ NIGIN₂ KUR in-neš-ši

(o i 4) (If) the Moon at its appearance is surrounded by 3 halos: the land will become confused.

o i 55

* 30 ina IGI.LA₂-šu₂ TUR₃ MUL-MEŠ NIGIN₂ ZIut BURU₅-ḪI!(GAR)⸢A⸣

(o i 5) (If) the Moon at its appearance is surrounded by a halo of stars: there will be an attack of locusts on the land and the land will be changed.

o i 66

a-na KUR GAL₂-ma KUR MAN!(ḪAL)ni

o i 77

* 30 ina IGI.LA₂-šu₂ TUR₃ dTIR.AN.NA NIGIN₂ ŠEG₃ NA₄ DU-ma

(o i 7) (If) the Moon at its appearance is surrounded by a rainbow halo: hail will fall and Adad will devastate (or): he will not devastate.

o i 88

dIŠKUR RAiṣ : NU RAiṣ

o i 99

* 30 ina IGI.LA₂-šu₂ TUR₃ MURU₉ NIGIN₂ BI₂.ZA.ZA

(o i 9) (If) the Moon at its appearance is surrounded by a halo of fog: frogs; clouds will stand in the sky.

o i 1010

DUNGU-MEŠ ana ANe <<KI>> ⸢GUB⸣-MEŠ

o i 1111

* 30 ina [IGI].LA₂-šu₂ TUR₃ me-ḫe-e NIGIN₂ ŠEG₃ ⸢NA₄⸣ DU-ma!(GIŠ)

(o i 11) Iif) the Moon at its appearance is surrounded by a halo of storm-wind: hail will fall but Adad will not devastate.

o i 1212

dIŠKUR NU RAiṣ

o i 1313

* 30 ina IGI.LA₂-šu₂ TUR₃ me-ḫe-e TU₁₅-1 NIGIN₂ ŠU.BI.AŠ.AM₃<(ŠEG₃)> ⸢NA₄⸣ DU-ma dIŠKUR NU RAiṣ

(o i 13) (If) the Moon at its appearance is surrounded by a halo of the south storm-wind: ditto (hail will fall by Adad will not devastate).

o i 1414

* 30 ina IGI.LA₂-šu₂ ul-la-nu-um-ma TUR₃ NIGIN₂ KUR ana KISLAḪ₃ NIGINḫur

(o i 14) (If) the Moon at its appearance is immediately surrounded by a halo: the land will gather into a fortress.

o i 1515

* 30 ⸢ina IGI⸣.LA₂-šu₂ 2 TUR₃ dTIR.AN.NA NIGIN₂ [...] ⸢LA₂⸣ ŠE u IN.NU

(o i 15) (If) the Moon at its appearance is surrounded by 2 rainbow haloes: [...] shortage of barley and straw.

o i 1616

* 30 ina IGI.[LA₂]-⸢šu₂ 2 TUR₃⸣ [...] x ŠE u ŠE.GIŠ.I₃ LA₂

(o i 16) (If) the Moon at its appearance [...] 2 haloes: ... barley and sesame will decrease; alternatively, ... [...] ... of straw; alternatively, there will be an attack of [...] against the land and it will be scattered.

o i 1717

KI.MIN x [...] x ⸢IN⸣.NU KI.MIN ZIut [...] ana KUR GAL₂-ma! BIR

o i 1818

[*] 30 ⸢ina IGI.LA₂-šu₂⸣ 2 TUR₃ tu₁₅[...] x NIGIN₂ x [...] IN.NU u ŠE.GIŠ.I₃

(o i 18) (If) the Moon at its appearance is surrounded by 2 haloes of [...] ...: [...] of straw and sesame.

o i 1919

[*] 30 ina IGI.[LA₂]-šu₂ 2 TUR₃ PI [...]-⸢MEŠ⸣ NIGIN₂ LA₂ ŠE u ŠE.GIŠ.I₃

(o i 19) (If) the Moon at its appearance is surrounded by 2 haloes of ... [...]: shortage of barley and sesame.

o i 2020

(o i 20) (If) the Moon at its appearance [...] a halo [...] Šulpae stands inside it: attack of the Amorites; great ... his servant in [...] and will seize the throne.

(o ii 11) (If) the Moon, as it brightens, stands in the knot of a lunar halo (and) a star stands in the space between [...]: the king, when he emerges, ... [...].

o ii 1717

* 30 TUR₃ DALḪAMUN KIN x NU ⸢NIGIN₂⸣ tu₁₅ŠEG₃ NA₄? DU-ma dIŠKUR x [...]

(o ii 17) (If) the Moon is surrounded by a dust-storm halo (and) ...: hail will fall and Adad ... [...].

o ii 1818

* 30 TUR₃ tu₁₅U₂.AN.⸢KI⸣.<NU.SA₂> [...] ⸢LA₂⸣ še-im ŠE.GIŠ.⸢I₃⸣

(o ii 18) (If) the Moon [...] a whirlwind halo: shortage of barley (and) sesame.

o ii 1919

* 30 GIŠ.ḪUR-MEŠ NIGIN⸢mi⸣ [(...)] ⸢LA₂⸣ še-im ŠE.GIŠ.⸢I₃⸣

(o ii 19) (If) the Moon is surrounded by cross-shaped haloes: shortages of barley (and) sesame.

o ii 2020

* 30 TUR₃ NIGIN₂-ma! ka-x [...] ⸢i-qa⸣-ru-⸢ur⸣

(o ii 20) (If) the Moon is surrounded by a halo and ... [...] shimmers: ... brother [...] ... will arise and sesame will thrive.

o ii 2121

x-ru ŠEŠ [...] x ZI-ma ŠE.GIŠ.I₃ ⸢GIŠ⸣

o ii 2222

[*] ⸢30⸣ TUR₃ NIGIN₂-ma ⸢i⸣-qar₃-ru-ur DUNGU-ME GAL₂-⸢ME⸣

(o ii 22) (If) the Moon is surrounded by a halo and it shimmers (and) there are clouds: [...] ... [...]... there will be the best of the harvest.

o ii 2323

[...] U₄ x [...] x IGI MU SIG₅ ⸢BURU₁₄ GAL₂⸣

o ii 2424

[...] ⸢NIGIN₂⸣-ma x x it-tap-ṭar tu₁₅ŠEG₃ x [...] x ZI x

(o ii 24) [...] is surrounded by [...] and ... has been cleared away: rain ... [...] ... will be released.

o ii 2525

[...] ⸢NIGIN₂⸣-ma KIN x x su-pi u₂-še-li? x x x x

o ii 2626

o ii 2727

(o ii 28) [...] surrounded by a black halo: there will be rain in the night.

* 30 [...] tu₁₅⸢ŠEG₃⸣ [...] ŠUB!ti KUR

(r i 2') (If) the Moon [...] rain [...] downfall of the land.

r i 3'3'

* 30 [... NIGIN₂]-⸢ma⸣ KA₂-šu₂ ana TU₁₅-2-šu₂ <<TU₁₅>> GAR ⸢ub-bu⸣-ṭu₂ ina KUR GAL₂

(r i 3') (If) the Moon is surrounded by [...] and its opening is located to its north: there will be starvation in the land.

r i 4'4'

* ⸢30⸣ [x] SA₅ ⸢NIGIN₂⸣-ma KA₂ la₃ TUKU ZI TUKU ⸢BAL⸣tu₄ SI.SA₂

(r i 4') (If) the Moon is surrounded by a red [...] and it does not have an opening: an uprising will succeed.

r i 5'5'

* 30 [x] SA₅ ⸢NIGIN₂⸣-ma KA₂-šu₂ ana TU₁₅-1 BAD RI AŠ BALtu₄ SI.SA₂

(r i 5') (If) the Moon is surrounded by a red [...] and its opening is open to the south: an uprising will succeed.

r i 6'6'

* ⸢30 TUR₃⸣ [x NIGIN₂]-⸢ma⸣ KA₂-šu₂ ana TU₁₅-2 <BAD> RI [x] TE GAR [...] x ⸢SI⸣.SA₂

r i 7'7'

r i 8'8'

(r i 8') (If) the Moon is surrounded by a red halo and its opening is open to the west: in heaven ... will succeed.
